name: Clytemnestra
description: Clytemnestra was the wife of Agamemnon, king of Mycenae, and sister to Helen of Troy. Her life was marked by tragedy and vengeance. Before the Trojan War, Agamemnon sacrificed their daughter, Iphigenia, to appease Artemis and ensure favorable winds for the Greek fleet. This act fueled Clytemnestra's burning hatred for her husband. While Agamemnon was away fighting in Troy for ten long years, Clytemnestra took Aegisthus as her lover and plotted her revenge. Upon Agamemnon's triumphant return, Clytemnestra, with Aegisthus's help, murdered him in his bath. She then ruled Mycenae with Aegisthus until her son, Orestes, returned to avenge his father's death and kill both Clytemnestra and Aegisthus. Clytemnestra is a complex figure, often portrayed as a strong, independent woman driven to violence by grief and injustice. She represents the destructive power of revenge and the consequences of betrayal, challenging traditional notions of wifely obedience and highlighting the cyclical nature of violence within the House of Atreus. Her story serves as a cautionary tale about the devastating effects of unchecked emotions and the enduring legacy of trauma.
subtitle: Queen of Mycenae, Driven by Revenge
achievements: Became a symbol of female rage

keyMyths.title: Sacrifice of Iphigenia
keyMyths.description: Agamemnon sacrificed his daughter, Iphigenia, to appease Artemis and allow the Greek fleet to sail to Troy. This act deeply scarred Clytemnestra and fueled her desire for revenge against her husband.

keyMyths.title: Murder of Agamemnon
keyMyths.description: Upon Agamemnon's return from Troy, Clytemnestra, with the help of her lover Aegisthus, murdered him in his bath. This act was driven by vengeance for Iphigenia's death and Agamemnon's infidelity.

keyMyths.title: Orestes' Revenge
keyMyths.description: Years later, Agamemnon and Clytemnestra's son, Orestes, returned to Mycenae and, urged by his sister Electra, killed Clytemnestra and Aegisthus to avenge his father's murder, fulfilling the cycle of violence.

sources.author: Aeschylus
sources.description: A trilogy of plays detailing the curse on the House of Atreus, including Agamemnon's murder and Orestes' revenge.
sources.text: A trilogy of plays detailing the curse on the House of Atreus, including Agamemnon's murder and Orestes' revenge.

sources.author: Euripides
sources.description: A play focusing on Electra's role in plotting Clytemnestra's death.
sources.text: A play focusing on Electra's role in plotting Clytemnestra's death.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: Brief mentions of Clytemnestra's betrayal and Agamemnon's murder upon his return from Troy.
sources.text: Brief mentions of Clytemnestra's betrayal and Agamemnon's murder upon his return from Troy.
